draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, responsive interactions over fluid animations when managing tight client timelines
facade.label: Instant snap vs smooth glide
facade.acceptImplies: CRM interactions should feel immediate and crisp; animations may feel wasteful given time-pressure context
facade.rejectImplies: Fluid, polished transitions matter more than raw speed; the overhead of smoothness builds confidence and care perception

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a friendly, conversational tone that feels like working with a supportive peer rather than enterprise software
facade.label: Casual and warm
facade.acceptImplies: User values approachability and human connection in their tools; may favor playful language, emojis, and personality in UI copy and notifications
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers a more formal, distanced, or corporate tone; may find overly casual language unprofessional or distracting from core functionality

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer snappy, responsive interactions over fluid animations when managing tight client timelines
evidence.implication: CRM interactions should feel immediate and crisp; animations may feel wasteful given time-pressure context

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high contrast between navigation and content areas for visual clarity and task focus
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values strong visual hierarchy and separation between workspace zones; likely wants dark mode support or high-contrast interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer subtle, uniform color schemes; possibly favors monochromatic or low-contrast aesthetics for a calmer workspace

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer glanceable client summaries (4-6 key metrics per card) over dense data tables for daily dashboard use
facade.label: Dashboard snapshot cards
facade.acceptImplies: Design the CRM with a card-based dashboard showing 4-6 essential metrics per client (revenue, last contact, pending deliverables) with visual hierarchy and color coding. Prioritize scannability over comprehensiveness.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore data-heavy approaches: full tables with sortable columns, detailed transaction logs, or unified spreadsheet views. Prioritize completeness and filtering power over at-a-glance clarity.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar paired with card-based layouts for clients/projects, reducing cognitive load when switching between detail views.
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and visual scanning over hierarchical navigation; likely to accept other compound layouts (sidebar + lists, sidebar + tables)
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer self-contained views (tabs, full-width) or flatter navigation; indicates possible preference for immersive focus over constant context visibility

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interfaces that feel personal and inviting rather than corporate and cold
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use warm colors (amber, terracotta, warm grays), soft shadows, and organic spacing to feel like a trusted workspace rather than enterprise software
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer cooler, crisper aesthetics—bright whites, cool blues, sharp shadows, minimal ornamentation—to signal efficiency and clarity

axes.label: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
Whether they want financial/metrics-heavy displays vs. task/project-heavy displays—reveals if they prioritize money tracking or work organization
Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a distraction-free workspace or one that feels energetic and branded
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they favor dense information dashboards vs. progressive disclosure—reveals if they want everything visible at once or prefer guided, step-by-step navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they respond to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want business-like efficiency or human, supportive guidance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer calm, minimal atmosphere vs. energetic, motivational atmosphere—reveals if they want zen focus or inspiring momentum
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they want financial/metrics-heavy displays vs. task/project-heavy displays—reveals if they prioritize money tracking or work organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether they prefer instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, considered transitions—reveals if they want speed and directness or polish and flow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers want tactile, gesture-based interactions for managing client lists and task priorities rather than menu-driven or click-heavy workflows, reducing cognitive load during rapid task switching
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture fluidity vs click precision
facade.label: Drag to sort
facade.acceptImplies: CRM should prioritize drag-and-drop, swipe, and gesture controls for core workflows like reordering clients, tasks, and invoices; fluid micro-interactions signal control and reduce friction
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers prefer explicit, click-based interactions with clear visual feedback over ambiguous gesture states; CRM should rely on buttons, menus, and confirmations to avoid accidental changes during fast work

draft.title: Freelancer CRM
draft.summary: A lightweight client and project management tool designed for independent freelancers to track leads, manage projects, and maintain client relationships.
